reviews.author: Kim Koch Rasmussen
reviews.text: Nice place to visit - it is more a jungle walk than a typical botanic garden. The longer route is said to take 3 hours - you can easily hike it in 1,5 hour. Longer if you stop for a swim... There is two places where you can swim. Together with the ticket you get a map - but the routes are very clearly marked along the path, so no chance of getting lost...

reviews.author: Ng Th Thong
reviews.text: Located within the Phong Nha – Ke Bang National Park, the Botanical Garden offers a perfect balance of light trekking, lush greenery, and relaxing natural scenery. The walking trails are well-maintained and easy to follow, making it suitable for visitors of different ages and fitness levels. One of the highlights is the beautiful waterfall and natural swimming area, where visitors can cool off and enjoy the clear, fresh water surrounded by forest. The garden is rich in plant life, with informative signs that introduce native trees and tropical vegetation, helping visitors better understand the local ecosystem. The atmosphere is calm and uncrowded compared to more adventurous caves, making it an ideal place for those who want to slow down and connect with nature. Staff members are friendly and helpful, ensuring a pleasant and safe visit. Overall, Phong Nha – Ke Bang Botanical Garden is a great choice for nature lovers, families, and travelers looking for a gentle outdoor experience. It’s a simple yet meaningful way to explore the biodiversity of this UNESCO World Heritage area.

reviews.author: Sergina Gerly
reviews.text: They doubled the entrance fee from 40k to 80k recently. A little exaggerated. The "big" hiking round doesn't take 3 hours as they say. It's 1 hour, maximum 1,5 hours to get to the waterfall doing the long route. Easy hike, even with sporty sandals, no hiking shoes necessary.

reviews.author: Plan•Travel•Eat•Repeat
reviews.text: We arrived later in the day so only had time for the small loop. It is more of a jungle Trek than a garden. However, I was in heaven. It was climbing over some rocks with rope to assist, some with trail and some with pave stone. There is basically a jungle you get to hike through to a waterfall. BUT it was amazing!! I only wish I had time for the big loop. Cost was 80,000 dong

reviews.author: Bram Delmot
reviews.text: We were here during the day but also at night with Ecofoot´s nightly jungle hike. It has its charm, especially with all those gazebos and old signs that transport you a bit into the past. At night there´s lots of frogs, spiders, gazebos, sleeping birds,... the waterfall is very nice too! We even saw a snake but don´t panic, this was at night and we were looking for it. You won´t encounter it just like that.

reviews.author: Casper Nielsen
reviews.text: Beautiful place to spend a few hours in nature! The Phong Nha Botanic Garden has a nice walking trail through the jungle, a small but lovely waterfall, and plenty of chances to spot wildlife. The paths are well-marked, and it’s not too crowded, which makes it feel peaceful and authentic. It’s a great way to experience the natural side of Phong Nha without needing a full-day cave tour. Bring water and good shoes — it can get hot, but it’s totally worth it!

reviews.author: Sam Rose
reviews.text: Very beautiful grounds and lovely water fall. We visited as a tour on the was to paradise cave and phong nha caves and would have liked to stay longer but we were restricted on time. Is a challenging climb down to the base of the waterfall and a couple on our trip slipped and hurt themselves

reviews.author: Yuri Moens
reviews.text: Not a botanic garden but a nice little hike through a jungle. Advertised as a 3h hike but even a couch potato like me did it in about 1h45 at a leisurely pace. There's 2 other routes you can take which are even shorter. The main attraction are the waterfalls and you can see them after about 15 minutes from the entrance. There's a really nice viewpoint at the top and the waterfalls themselves are quite nice. If you do the full path it's highly likely that your shoes will get wet here. Doing the full tour will also guide you past a river and swimming hole where you can... well swim. The only other attractions are 2 100 year old trees. Are they worth it? No. I mean they're nice trees but there's nothing special about them. I did enjoy the hike though so I can recommend that. Just don't do it for the trees. The trail itself is easy. There's a clear path, it's marked, and the slightly more difficult parts have ropes to help you out. Unless you are in really bad shape you will have no problem hiking here. Price per person is 80k, not 40k as Google shows currently. 50-60k would be a bit fairer for this place but it's still not too bad.

reviews.author: Bagigu Philip
reviews.text: The waterfall is nice but getting to it, other than directly from the entrance side, can be challenging and slippery. Some will surely like it like that. There's a viewpoint you can climb to that you see only the trees around you. No view whatsoever. Can be a pleasant way to spend some time and entrance fee is not much.

reviews.author: Vlad B
reviews.text: OK hike loop through the waterfall. Nothing dramatic, you may have to take off your shoes or wear waterproof shoes to cross the waterfall depending on water level that day. There is a large swing after the waterfall that is nice and you can push off the tree to go really high.

reviews.author: Marci Thrall
reviews.text: You can skip this one. First: botanic garden indicates flowers where I come from. No flowers. Second: there are literally thousands of places in Vietnam and in this area that are worth spending an hour or two. This ain’t it. The “lake” is a puddle. The “viewpoint” is overgrown with no views, the waterfall is meh. There is just as much lush greenery everywhere else you go.

reviews.author: A. McDougall
reviews.text: This is a great place to hike, with a clear path and signs. The longest open trail will take a moderately fit person no more than 90 minutes. The longer loop is closed, so you will end up going to the falls, then climbing into the forest, which is quiet and so lush. There are no snacks or drinks available, so gear up. 60,000 entrance fee

reviews.author: Megaworld Asia
reviews.text: This place is promoted as a Botanical Garden, but it isn’t. If you want to know what a real botanical garden looks like, go to Singapore. What’s here is a tropical rain forest, covering a karst landscape, with hiking trails and some nice sightseeing attractions. The entry fee is 45,000 VND. This allows you to do the YELLOW TRAIL. There’s a map in the entrance area, which provides detail of the route. Most visitors just go to GIO WATERFALL, which is only 400 meters from the ticket office. If you want to do the full yellow trail hike, I highly recommend going in the opposite direction (counterclockwise) to the information map. The reason being is that you will end the hike at the swimming hole and be able to go for cooling swim. If you go in the direction shown on the map (clockwise) you’ll walk past the swimming hole at the beginning of the hike. Go to the waterfall first, then make the ascent up the most difficult part of the trail, to the viewpoint. After this it’s mostly downhill until you arrive at the swimming hole. Note: the best time of the year to visit is April – June, when there’s still water flowing. Safe travels, Mega.

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: Such a peaceful and lush spot to explore just outside of town. The gardens are beautifully maintained and incredibly green, with lots of plant varieties clearly labeled and spread across well-marked trails. The walking paths are a mix of stone steps and dirt tracks, and while it can be a bit slippery after rain, it’s still very walkable with proper shoes. The bamboo bridges crossing small creeks really added to the jungle charm, and there’s a great mix of natural scenery throughout the trail – including a waterfall that makes for an awesome photo stop. It’s quiet, not too busy, and definitely a refreshing change of pace. I’d recommend setting aside 90 to 120 minutes to enjoy the experience without rushing. If you’re in Phong Nha and enjoy nature or a bit of light trekking, this is absolutely worth adding to your list. 💵 Entry: 80,000 VND per adult 🛵 Parking: 5,000 VND for motorbikes, 10,000 VND for cars 👟 Tip: Wear sturdy shoes, especially if it’s been raining

reviews.author: Robert Speller
reviews.text: Went here as part of a day trip but it's not worth it if wanting to go separately. Small building holding taxidermist animals from the forest. Trek a short way to a waterfall that is ok for some pics. We did see a large squirrel on the way back

reviews.author: Emma Freer
reviews.text: Beautiful spot to see the waterfall. You do need to clamber over rocks to get to it. Not for less mobile people.

reviews.author: Heni
reviews.text: Beautiful nature walk. Be prepared for uneven terrain! The restaurant has fair prices and the food tastes great. Would definitely recommend!

reviews.author: Raman Nagpal
reviews.text: The reason why i gave 4 stars is that we could not go much inside as our guide dis not let us do that... the place we just went was till waterfall... the way was nice and seemed like full of adventure, but adventure ended in just 40 min. Though we clicked few nice pictures of nature but couldn't do much

reviews.author: Rachel
reviews.text: Amazing find! Easy to self navigate while also fun and adventurous. 40k entry to the park and a nice little cafe at the entrance. Not the most incredible in terms of views for the area but amazing not to be charged $90 per person to go on a hike!!

reviews.author: Kym Kym
reviews.text: One of my favorite gems in the area that needs no guide, just drive up on your motorcycle and enjoy

reviews.author: Han Luka
reviews.text: 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟 A Peaceful and Educational Nature Experience Visiting the Phong Nha Botanical Garden was a truly refreshing experience. The lush greenery, well-maintained trails, and informative signboards throughout the garden made it an ideal spot for nature lovers, families, and travelers seeking a quiet escape. I appreciated how the garden is organized to allow visitors to learn about the local flora while enjoying scenic walks under the shade of tall trees. It’s also a great place to spot birds and butterflies if you take your time. The facilities are clean, and the staff were helpful and friendly. There’s also a small waterfall and natural swimming area inside, which adds an adventurous touch to the visit. Highly recommended for anyone coming to Phong Nha–Ke Bang National Park. It’s not just a garden – it’s a peaceful space to reconnect with nature and learn something new.

reviews.author: Omz (Omz)
reviews.text: Quite an enjoyable visit. Similar to another reviewer here I made the mistake of assuming this would be a traditional botanic garden (my bad there in not doing the due diligence). Instead, it offers a forest or jungle walk experience which was quite nice albeit rather hot and humid (in April): The surroundings are beautiful, but it's important to note that the longer walk can be challenging if you’re not used to walking in humid weather. The view from the waterfall was quite picturesque and quite a few folks took a dip in the pool. The viewing point, however, may not offer as rewarding a view and can be difficult to access, making it less worthwhile. Overall quite a decent place and well worth the visit

reviews.author: Reuben
reviews.text: This is a really awesome place. There's a few different hiking options to choose from. The full loop is about 3 hours or you can take the shortcut route which is about 1.5 hours. Or you can just go straight to the waterfall which is about 20 min each way. Hiking through the jungle is really cool and I wish I had time to go back there again.

reviews.author: Stef Dowie
reviews.text: Really cool jungle vibes. Easy to follow route around the garden. There are two parts where you can take a swin, including at the beautiful big waterfall. Small loop takes about 1.5hrs and big loop up to 3hrs. Entrance fee super reasonable, only 40k

reviews.author: Karly Feinberg
reviews.text: This was such a fun place to visit, but it felt more like a regular park as opposed to a botanical garden. There are different hikes of varying lengths. The shortest is about 30 min and the longest one is said to be 3 hours but we finished it in maybe half that time? There are two swimming spots which were a lot of fun but bring inspect repellant!!! When we initially arrived we ended up leaving and coming back with pants and insect repellant because as soon as we stepped on the trail we were completely surrounded by mosquitoes.

reviews.author: Andy Mc
reviews.text: 17 minutes from Phong Nha by car or scooter, don't try it by bicycle because you'll end up pushing it for the last 1.2km. Toilets and cafe by entrance and only 40k for the entrance ticket. There is a short route of 1.5 hrs and a longer route of 3 hrs. We chose the longer route and it was worth the effort. Wear stout footwear, splash on the muzzy repellent, pack extra water and wear trunks etc if you fancy a cool off in the pool at the bottom of the waterfall.

reviews.author: Hunor Gocz
reviews.text: It is a lovely botanical garden. I recommend it. There are more trails to choose from: - the shortest one goes directly to the waterfall (roundtrip takes around 40 min) - the shortest circuit is about 1.5 hours long - the longest circuit is about 2.5 hours long In practice they can be done faster too but these estimates they give is for walking with no rush and stop for pictures. The trails are easy to do, not a hard hike at all.

reviews.author: Ben
reviews.text: The Botanic Gardens, while enjoyable to visit, might not meet expectations as a traditional botanic garden. Instead, it offers a forest or jungle walk experience. The surroundings are beautiful, but it's important to note that the longer walk can be challenging. The view from the waterfall is picturesque, offering a glimpse into the jungle, but the viewing point may not offer as rewarding a view and can be difficult to access, making it less worthwhile. Personally, I found cycling around the area and exploring nature to be a more fulfilling experience, but this preference may vary from person to person.

reviews.author: Bart van de Ven
reviews.text: Althougt its more of a nature walk than an botanical garden it was still very pleasant to go there for a walk and when you arrive to the waterfall its was simply amazing! We even went for a little swim in the clear water pond in the middle of the waterfall which was super refreshing! Would recommend this place as its only 40k dong entrance per person and 5k dong to park your bike :)

reviews.author: GG
reviews.text: Nice trail with stunning waterfalls. Not suitable for all as path is very steep and slippery with some climbing.
